items.title: Add 'No hidden fees' statement near rental form CTA
items.description: Competitors (Triangle Lawn Games, Reventals) explicitly advertise 'no extra fees' as a selling point because customers are often burned by surprise charges. Adding a single line 'No hidden fees — total shown before you confirm' near the form submit button directly addresses the #1 reason customers abandon rental bookings.
items.source: Competitor analysis: trianglelawngames.com, reventals.com; cart abandonment research: foursixty.com/blog

items.title: Show live total price in rental form as dates are selected
items.description: When a customer picks rental dates in the form, the total price (pricePerDay × days) should update in real time and be prominently displayed before the submit button. Research shows dynamic price transparency is the single biggest checkout conversion lever for rental services.
items.source: UX research: unicornplatform.com/blog/car-rental-booking-pages-in-2026; Baymard checkout optimization

items.title: Category filter chips overflow on mobile without horizontal scroll
items.description: On 375px screens the 6 category pills wrap onto two rows or overflow, breaking the layout. The filter bar needs a horizontally scrollable single row with touch scrolling on mobile — common pattern used by Airbnb, Etsy, and all major marketplace apps.
items.source: PRODUCT.md mobile-first principle; Smashing Magazine booking UX case study

items.title: All game images need descriptive alt text
items.description: Screen reader users receive no information about game appearance since images have empty or generic alt attributes. Each game card image should have an alt like 'Giant Jenga — oversized wooden block tower stacked outdoors'. Required for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
items.source: WCAG 2.1 AA audit guidance: boia.org/blog/web-accessibility-for-travel-agencies; accessibe.com accessible rental properties

items.title: Category filter and player chip groups need keyboard navigation
items.description: Filter chip groups should follow the ARIA radio group pattern so keyboard users can Tab to the group and use arrow keys to change selection. Currently a keyboard-only user must Tab through every individual button. Required for WCAG 2.1 AA.
items.source: WCAG 2.1 accessible booking flow guidelines: frontdesksuite.com/en/product/accessibility

items.title: Show cancellation policy on game modal before rental form
items.description: Customers want to know cancellation terms before committing to fill out a form. Competitors like bookextraordinary.com advertise 'free cancellation up to 24 hours' prominently. Adding a short cancellation policy line in the GameModal builds confidence at the decision stage.
items.source: Competitor analysis: bookextraordinary.com; rental UX: techspokes.com/blog/vacation-rental-website-conversions

items.title: Event package bundles (e.g. 'Party Pack: 3 games for $X/day')
items.description: Multiple competitor sites (bookextraordinary.com, reventals.com) offer curated event packages bundling 3-4 complementary games at a slight discount. This increases average order value and helps indecisive customers. Could be implemented as a featured section above the game grid with hardcoded bundles.
items.source: Competitor analysis: bookextraordinary.com, reventals.com/blog/lawn-game-rental-ideas
items.pmVerdict: rejected: bundle pricing requires multi-game discount logic outside the per-day pricing model; out of current product scope and risks scope creep into the discount/promo system explicitly excluded in PRODUCT.md

items.title: Inline form field validation (not just on submit)
items.description: Research from Baymard shows that forms validating only on submit cause 22% more errors and take longer to complete. Each field in the rental form should validate on blur — name required, email format, phone format — with inline green/red feedback so customers correct errors as they go.
items.source: Baymard Institute checkout UX; alfdesigngroup.com/post/checkout-ux; quape.com/checkout-ux-cart-abandonment

items.title: Game modal 'What's included' section (setup, pickup/delivery, accessories)
items.description: Customers frequently ask what exactly they're getting with a rental — is setup included? What accessories come with the game? Adding a short bullet list 'Included: boards, 8 bean bags, carrying bag — delivery available' inside the GameModal removes a key barrier to booking.
items.source: Customer pain point: vendor communication frustration (reventals.com customer research); competitor UX patterns

items.title: Rental form has no inline date validation (past dates accepted)
items.description: The current rental form date inputs allow selecting past dates or end dates before start dates without any feedback. This creates confusing behaviour and erroneous orders. Date fields should validate: start ≥ today, end ≥ start + 1, with inline error messages.
items.source: Equipment rental UX: eliftech.com/insights/how-to-improve-booking-experience; hotel booking engine problems: traveltekpro.com

items.title: Add 'Most Popular' badge to top games
items.description: Social proof badges ('Most Popular', 'Fan Favourite', 'Perfect for weddings') on 2-3 game cards reduce decision paralysis. Gigantogames.com and competitors use these prominently. Can be implemented as a hardcoded badge field on the Game type.
items.source: Competitor analysis: gigantogames.com; trust signal research: mobiloud.com/blog/trust-signals-cart-abandonment

items.title: Game modal backdrop click accidentally dismisses the modal
items.description: Clicking the dark overlay around the game modal closes it. Users reading game details or filling dates tap the edges of the modal and lose their context. Industry standard is to require explicit close button click or Escape key to dismiss modals with form state.
items.source: UX best practice; e-commerce modal patterns: uxpin.com/studio/blog/web-layout-best-practices
items.pmVerdict: rejected: backlog is healthy at 25+ pending stories; this is a medium-confidence UX preference (some users expect backdrop-to-close) rather than a clear usability defect — defer to a future run when priority stories are cleared

items.title: Quick 'days' selector on game card showing instant total price
items.description: Add a simple '1 day / 2 days / Weekend' toggle directly on each game card that instantly shows the total price without opening the modal. Car rental sites (Hertz, Enterprise) show this as their primary conversion mechanic — customers convert 30% faster when they see total price before clicking.
items.source: Car rental booking UX: unicornplatform.com/blog/car-rental-booking-pages-in-2026; e-commerce UX: digitalapplied.com/blog/ecommerce-checkout-optimization-2026
items.pmVerdict: rejected: medium confidence + medium effort; US-021 already provides live price in the rental form; adding a redundant price widget on the card risks cluttering the grid UI; defer until post-US-021 user testing reveals a gap

items.title: Skeleton loading state for game grid on initial page load
items.description: The game grid renders all at once after JS hydration, causing a flash of unstyled content on slow connections. Adding a CSS skeleton loader (pulsing grey card outlines) prevents layout shift and makes the page felt faster. Required by PRODUCT.md design principle 5.
items.source: PRODUCT.md design principle 5: 'no layout shift on load'; core web vitals CLS best practices

items.title: Add service area / delivery radius notice in the hero section
items.description: Customers landing on the site don't know if the service covers their area. Adding 'Serving [City/Region] and surrounding areas' in the hero prevents customers from spending time building a booking only to discover at checkout there is no delivery to their location.
items.source: Competitor pattern: all competitor sites prominently show service city (dallaslawngames.com, sanantoniolawngames.com); customer frustration research
items.pmVerdict: accepted: static informational text in the hero, not delivery tracking or logistics; addresses a key early-funnel conversion barrier; feasible as a shared constant in Next.js

items.title: Floating 'Book Now' sticky CTA on mobile product browsing
items.description: On mobile, the 'Rent Now' button inside GameModal is only visible after scrolling down. A sticky bottom bar with 'Rent [Game Name] — $X/day — Book Now' pinned to the bottom of the modal on mobile keeps the conversion action always visible without requiring scroll.
items.source: Mobile conversion optimization: parallelhq.com/blog/ux-design-to-improve-website-conversions; mobile-first booking UX: goodshuffle.com/blog/event-rental-website-builder
items.pmVerdict: rejected: run cap of 5 stories reached; medium confidence — sticky bars on modals can cause z-index conflicts and layout issues; defer to next PM run after existing modal stories (US-025, US-027) are dev-complete so the interaction can be assessed together

items.title: Rental form inputs missing visible focus rings and ARIA error IDs
items.description: Form inputs lose their focus ring in some browsers (removed via outline:none), making keyboard navigation invisible. Each input also needs aria-describedby pointing to its error message element for screen reader users. Required for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
items.source: WCAG 2.1 form accessibility guidelines; accessible booking: simplyscheduleappointments.com/accessibility-booking-plugin
items.pmVerdict: rejected: duplicate of US-030 (Rental form inputs: visible focus rings and ARIA error associations) which already covers focus ring restoration and aria-describedby linkage for all form inputs

items.title: 'Games for events' quick-filter links on homepage (Wedding, Corporate, Kids Party)
items.description: Competitors use occasion-based navigation ('Wedding games', 'Corporate events', 'Kids birthday') as a primary discovery path. Adding 3 occasion-based quick-filter buttons above the game grid lets customers find relevant games by use case instead of scrolling through all categories.
items.source: Competitor UX: bookextraordinary.com, rusticvibegames.com; event planning trends: tentandtable.net/blog/post/trends-in-party-rentals
items.pmVerdict: accepted: occasion-based discovery is a genuine new capability not covered by existing category/player/search filters; feasible as client-side tag filtering with a new optional field on Game type; addresses real competitor gap

items.title: Empty state in game grid should include a CTA to contact or browse all
items.description: When filters return zero results, the 'No games found' message is a dead end — no link back to all games, no call to action. Per PRODUCT.md design principle 3 ('never a dead end'), the zero-results state needs a 'Browse all games' button and optionally a 'Request a custom game' link.
items.source: PRODUCT.md design principle; e-commerce empty state UX best practices

items.title: Add game dimensions and weight to the modal so customers know what to expect
items.description: Customers booking for events need to know if a game fits their space. The games.ts already has a dimensions field but GameModal doesn't visually highlight it. A prominent 'Setup space needed: 10ft × 10ft' callout with a simple icon would directly answer a key pre-booking question.
items.source: Customer pain point research: uncertainty about physical setup requirements; competitor analysis showing setup details prominently
items.pmVerdict: rejected: duplicate of US-032 (Setup space callout in game modal) which already covers a prominent dimensions callout with icon inside GameModal reusing the existing dimensions field

items.title: Testimonials section has no link to more reviews or review source
items.description: The testimonials section shows 3 hardcoded reviews with no source attribution (no Google, Yelp, or similar), making them look fake to skeptical customers. At minimum, adding a star rating and a city name to each testimonial increases perceived authenticity.
items.source: Trust signal research: mobiloud.com/blog/trust-signals-cart-abandonment; B2C trust patterns for service businesses
items.pmVerdict: accepted: PRODUCT.md explicitly states 'trust signals matter'; small effort change to static data that increases social proof authenticity; no external services or analytics required

items.title: Micro-event 'Backyard Party' bundle suggestion based on cart contents
items.description: The 2025 micro-events trend (smaller backyard gatherings) means customers often want 2-3 complementary games. After a customer adds a game to the cart or opens a modal, show 'Complete your backyard party: customers who rented [Giant Jenga] also rented [Cornhole Set]'. Implemented purely in-memory, no backend needed.
items.source: Event planning trends 2025-2026: tentandtable.net/blog/post/trends-in-party-rentals, accio.com/business/event-rental-trends; e-commerce cross-sell patterns
items.pmVerdict: rejected: medium confidence + medium effort; backlog is healthy at 27 pending stories; cross-sell recommendation logic adds complexity to cart/modal interaction that should be deferred until the cart (US-008) is dev-complete and user-tested; risk of clutter in modal alongside US-025 and US-027
